brief_document.title: Live outbox reconciliation sweep: no-restart re-arm of stranded claimed rows
brief_document.purpose: The durable outbox re-arms stranded rows ONLY on server restart (rearm_outbox_pending during recovery). If a row is flipped to 'claimed' by the OutboxDispatcher and then its dispatch target dies (worker crash / network partition) without reaching done/failed/retry, the row stays 'claimed' indefinitely until the next restart — delivery for that ordinal stalls. Add a LIVE, in-process reconciliation sweep that detects stranded 'claimed' rows past a threshold and re-arms them (back to pending) WITHOUT a restart, preserving exactly-once. NOTE (verified): the outbox table currently has NO claimed_at/lease timestamp, so staleness is not yet detectable — adding that is the first prerequisite. The OBX-012 e2e harness already proves the happy-path + restart-recovery contract this builds on.
brief_document.task: Add a live reconciliation sweep for the durable outbox. (1) Add a durable claim timestamp to the outbox table (e.g. claimed_at, RFC3339), set when a row is claimed, via a backward-compatible DDL migration (ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS or equivalent) in the libsql schema. (2) Add an OutboxStore method to find/re-arm rows in 'claimed' status whose claim is older than a threshold (e.g. reclaim_claimed_before(as_of) or list_claimed_before(as_of)), reusing the existing rearm semantics (status -> pending, visible_after = now, attempt PRESERVED). (3) Spawn a reconciliation task as a SIBLING of the OutboxDispatcher in run_server's outbox bootstrap (maybe_spawn_outbox_dispatcher), sharing the shutdown watch and the engine's single libsql connection, gated behind explicit config (reconciliation interval + staleness threshold, following the existing opt-in outbox.enabled / no-silent-default pattern). The sweep SHALL scan ONLY 'claimed' rows (never racing the dispatcher's pending-claim path) and re-arm stale ones. Exactly-once MUST be preserved: a re-armed + re-dispatched ordinal whose original in-flight attempt later completes is safely de-duplicated by the existing Recorder completion-dedup (record_fan_out_completion drops a terminal for an already-resolved ordinal). Do NOT implement cross-node completion routing (that is a separate later item); this is recovery of stranded in-flight dispatch only.

requirements.title: Durable claim timestamp + stale-claim store method
requirements.spec: THE outbox row SHALL carry a durable claim timestamp set when the row is claimed. THE OutboxStore SHALL expose a method to re-arm (or list for re-arming) rows in 'claimed' status whose claim timestamp is older than a caller-supplied instant, using the existing re-arm semantics (status -> pending, visible_after = now, attempt preserved). The schema change SHALL be backward-compatible (additive, IF NOT EXISTS).
acceptance: outbox table has a claim timestamp column set on claim_outbox_rows; migration is additive and safe on an existing DB.
A new OutboxStore method re-arms (or lists) 'claimed' rows older than a given instant; attempt count is preserved across re-arm.
Rows in done/failed are NEVER touched by the sweep; only stale 'claimed' rows are re-armed.
Unit/store tests cover: a fresh claim is NOT reclaimed; a claim older than threshold IS reclaimed; a done/failed row is untouched.

requirements.title: Live reconciliation task wired into run_server, exactly-once preserved
requirements.spec: WHEN outbox + reconciliation are enabled, THE SYSTEM SHALL run a periodic in-process sweep (no restart) that re-arms stranded 'claimed' rows past the staleness threshold, and the stranded fan-out members SHALL still complete EXACTLY ONCE (a late completion from the original in-flight attempt is dropped by the Recorder dedup; no duplicate terminal events, no double-wake).
acceptance: A reconciliation task is spawned beside the OutboxDispatcher in run_server's outbox bootstrap, sharing the shutdown watch + libsql connection, gated by explicit config (interval + threshold; opt-in, no silent default).
The sweep scans only 'claimed' rows and does not race / contend with the dispatcher's pending-claim transaction.

An integration test simulates a stranded claim (a claimed row whose dispatch never completes), runs the live sweep (no restart), and asserts the member is re-dispatched and completes exactly once with exactly one terminal in history.
A duplicate/late completion for an already-resolved ordinal is dropped (not double-recorded).

boundaries: EXACTLY-ONCE IS THE INVARIANT: re-arm + re-dispatch MUST NOT produce duplicate terminal events or double-wake; rely on the existing Recorder completion-dedup (record_fan_out_completion / ordinal_is_resolved). If you cannot preserve it, REPORT rather than weaken it.
SHALL NOT change the existing claim/complete/retry/fail semantics or the restart-path rearm_outbox_pending behaviour; the live sweep reuses the same re-arm transition.
SHALL NOT implement cross-node completion routing / the continue-as-new RunId-on-wire work (separate items #10/#11); scope is stranded in-flight DISPATCH recovery only.
The sweep SHALL scan only 'claimed' rows and MUST NOT contend with or duplicate the dispatcher's pending-claim path (single shared libsql connection; keep sweep queries minimal).
Schema migration MUST be additive + safe on an existing populated DB (no destructive change).
Reconciliation MUST be opt-in via explicit config (interval + threshold), following the existing outbox.enabled no-silent-default pattern; disabled by default.
SHALL NOT introduce unsafe code; SHALL keep no production unwrap()/expect()/panic!() (workspace denies); files under 500 lines; mod.rs only pub use.
verification: cargo fmt --check clean for changed files (rustfmt edition 2024; --config skip_children=true).
cargo test passes in full including new store unit tests + the live-sweep integration test; zero failures.
The integration test genuinely exercises the live sweep (fails if the reconciler is disabled/removed) and the exactly-once dedup (fails if a duplicate terminal is recorded).
The schema migration applies cleanly to a pre-existing outbox DB (no data loss).
No new public API surface beyond the OutboxStore method + opt-in config the reconciler needs.

constraints.text: No file over 500 lines; extract submodules if approaching the limit.
No panics in production code; unwrap()/expect() only in tests.
mod.rs contains only pub mod / pub use; no logic.
All public types implement Debug; all error types implement std::error::Error.

resolved_context.intention: Outbox hardening #9: a no-restart, live reconciliation sweep that re-arms stranded 'claimed' outbox rows (dead worker / partition) so delivery does not stall until a restart — exactly-once preserved via the Recorder dedup. Prerequisite (verified): the outbox table has no claim timestamp today, so add one. Builds on the OBX-012 e2e contract. See docs/AION-OUTBOX-BUILD-PLAN.md and ABLATIVE-STACK-ROADMAP.md. SENSITIVE distributed code — correctness over cleverness.

provenance.quote: keep on going on those things so long as everything is properly documented
